summary: No-ship: the new souls hint path trusts more than the stated `{taskType,tier}` contract and can also turn a bad routing config into request-time 500s for otherwise valid `/chat` calls.

findings.title: Souls-mode hint now honors caller-supplied `matchedException`, letting clients bypass the matrix and force exception routing
findings.recommendation: Do not carry `matchedException` through from `body.classification`; only honor validated `taskType`/`tier` and let exception matching remain internal to the router/classifier. At minimum, add a regression test that a body with `{taskType,tier,matchedException}` resolves exactly as `{taskType,tier}`.

findings.title: A valid explicit hint can now crash souls `/chat` at request time if the loaded routing config is internally inconsistent
findings.recommendation: Wrap `resolveRoute` in a local try/catch and fall back to `null`, or validate the loaded routing config up front before enabling hint handling. This preserves the slice requirement that bad routing state must not break existing souls-mode behavior.
